items.title: BreastScreen SA did not get ethics approval or consent before needle study, ombudsman finds

children.content: South Australia's public breast cancer screening service conducted research without ethics approval or consent from almost 100 patients, who it asked to do a second breast needle biopsy, prompting the service to "unreservedly" apologise.

gallery.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

thumbnail.altText: The office of BreastScreen SA in Adelaide.

items.title: 'Attention-seeking' man allegedly targeted police, defence in 'cybercrime spree'

children.content: A 22-year-old Adelaide man who allegedly went on a "cybercrime spree", targeting several government departments and a courthouse, will remain behind bars until at the least the end of the year.

image.altText: Close up image of the face of a man with Asian heritage

items.title: Airport workers angered by surprise Qantas route cuts

children.content: Qantas's decision to cut one regional South Australian route and suspend four others came as a shock to workers at regional airports.

image.altText: A QantasLink plane on the tarmac

items.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

items.title: 'No-one's committed murder here': Port CEO urges change to tribunal process

children.content: Port Adelaide's chief executive has slammed the AFL Tribunal process, labelling it "too legalistic" and "too adversarial".

image.altText: A man is a suit is frowning as he looks toward his left side.

items.title: SA government has 'assurances' from LIV Golf as course upgrade under fresh scrutiny

children.content: The South Australian government has reaffirmed its commitment to a $45 million upgrade of the North Adelaide public golf course to host LIV Golf in 2028, and has "assurances" about the tour's future.

articles.title: Is LIV Golf dead? Not yet, but the vultures are circling

children.content: Rumours of LIV Golf's immediate demise look wide of the mark, but the smoke is only growing thicker as denials from the league's CEO help paint a bleak picture.

image.altText: Cameron Smith at a LIV Golf press conference

articles.title: LIV Golf season to continue 'full throttle' amid funding speculation

children.content: LIV Golf will run as planned in 2026 according to the league's CEO, as media reports suggest its funding from Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund may soon end.

image.altText: Golf executive standing on the first tee, looking into the distance

gallery.altText: Two flags on a golf course with large buildings from the city in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Two flags on a golf course with large buildings from the city in the background.

items.title: SA government apologises to prisoner denied abortion medication

children.content: The South Australian ombudsman calls for changes to medical monitoring at the Adelaide Women's Prison after a female prisoner was denied a medical abortion, which the health minister says is unacceptable.

image.altText: A building surrounded by a fence and trees

items.title: Australia's rarest reptile found after being considered extinct

children.content: Not found anywhere else on Earth, a skink considered functionally extinct has been found in a national park in far west NSW.

image.altText: A skink on a large rock

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: AFL 'firestorm' taking heavy toll on players, Saints coach says

children.content: St Kilda coach Ross Lyon says the damage from a "firestorm" around the AFL Tribunal is weighing heavily as his club signals it may appeal against Lance Collard's suspension.

image.altText: A St Kilda AFL coach stands on the ground looking downfield ahead of a match.

items.title: The outback jobs disappearing as fuel costs hit tourism

children.content: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

image.altText: A young woman bottle feeds a calf and a lamb.

items.title: In charts: A look at Australia's record spending on defence

children.content: The Defence Department reveals where the money will be going as Australia plans to spend a record $425 billion over the next decade.

image.altText: Minister for Defence Industry Pat Conroy and Deputy Prime Minister Richard Marles in front of a submarine.

items.title: Unpacking the 'war zone' after Geelong's refinery fire

children.content: The "unprecedented" fire at one of the two remaining oil refineries in Australia took more than 12 hours to extinguish. Here's what we know about how it unfolded.

articles.title: Poor communication, no updates during oil refinery fire, residents claim

children.content: Geelong residents near an oil refinery fire have told a community meeting that initial information from emergency services was misleading and there was a lack of updates.

image.altText: Smoke near a refinery.

items.title: Australia is on the verge of aged care AI boom

children.content: Artificial intelligence-enabled robot companions, personal behaviour monitors and pain management apps could soon be used more widely within the sector.

image.altText: A close shot of a husband and wife holding each others hands.

items.title: Liberals need to resolve internal questions on migration approach

children.content: The Liberals need to resolve the internal ambiguities they have on big policy questions when it comes to migration.

image.altText: Angus Taylor stands behind Jonno Duniam.

items.title: 'Never thought we'd be homeless': Mother of 13 pleads for help

children.content: Finding a home for a large family in a regional area has left one family camping on the side of the highway in rural New South Wales. Nationally, Indigenous people are over-represented in homelessness statistics.

gallery.altText: A boy eats his noodles in a camp chair.

thumbnail.altText: A boy eats his noodles in a camp chair.

items.title: Coca-Cola flags it could pull out of Qld recycling scheme

children.content: The Queensland government recently introduced proposed laws to overhaul the governance of the scheme, while also ruling out an increase to the 10-cent refund amount.

image.altText: Coke cans at a WA containers for change depot

items.title: AFL-backed helmet company erases concussion protection claims

children.content: An AFL-endorsed helmet company has backtracked on its claims of "superior protection against concussion" and the league is now also facing a complaint lodged with the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission on the matter.

image.altText: A black padded sports helmet on a white background.

items.title: Decision looms as Ben Roberts-Smith expected to apply for bail

children.content: Australia's most decorated living soldier has spent more than a week in custody. Today in court, he is expected to make a bid to be released on bail after being charged with war crimes.

image.altText: Ben Roberts Smith in a blue suit, white shirt, outside court on a sunny day.

items.title: 'Same general, new clothes': Civilian facade as military hides wealth in Thailand

children.content: As the Myanmar military carried out air strikes during a brutal civil war, the junta chief's family quietly purchased luxury real estate in Thailand.

image.altText: man in yellow silk cap.

items.title: Weekly news quiz: Trump's AI image, a 200m record and the Artemis II splashdown

children.content: It's Friday, and that means it's time to test your news knowledge from the past seven days. Were you paying attention this week?

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in NASA space suits, wearing caps saying Artemis 2 smile at the camera

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in NASA space suits, wearing caps saying Artemis 2 smile at the camera

items.title: Ephemeral nature of fungi has this photographer hooked on forest life

children.content: An exhibition bridges the gap between art and science by inviting visitors to imagine the world through the senses of other species and consider our own fragile relationship with nature.

image.altText: A cluster of cup-shaped mushrooms cling to a damp fallen branch amonst feathery ferns.

items.title: Invasive species you should avoid — and what to plant instead

children.content: Agapanthus, morning glory or gazanias might be easy-growing plants in your home but their invasive nature can damage your garden and the environment. Here's what experts recommend.

image.altText: A woman with black curly hair walking through a door outside with a plant pot in hand.

items.title: Prince Harry tells summit he's 'felt lost, betrayed, or completely powerless’

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex are back together in Melbourne after spending the second day of their Australian tour apart.

gallery.altText: Prince Harry delivering a speech at a lecturn.
Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex arrive to participate in the Scar Tree Walk at Birrarung Marr.

thumbnail.altText: Prince Harry delivering a speech at a lecturn.
Prince Harry and Meghan, the Duke and Duchess of Sussex arrive to participate in the Scar Tree Walk at Birrarung Marr.

items.title: Trbojevic injures hamstring as Manly thrash Cowboys

children.content: The Sea Eagles have smashed North Queensland but the win has come at a heavy cost following a hamstring injury to star fullback Tom Trbojevic.

image.altText: Tom Trbojevic looks despondent after suffering an injury

items.title: Daicos inspires Magpies to win as Blues youngster misses kick after siren

children.content: Nick Daicos stars in the final quarter to inspire Collingwood to a win as Carlton youngster Talor Byrne misses a kick after the siren to tie the scores.

image.altText: Nick Daicos celebrates a goal with teammates

items.title: Mandelson failed vetting but was given UK ambassador to US job anyway

children.content: Peter Mandelson was sacked from the role after revelations about his connections with the late sex offender became public.

image.altText: Peter Mandelson, wearing a blue suit, speaks in front of the UK and US flags.

items.title: US alliance indispensable as Marles looks to build more self-sufficient ADF

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has declared the United States alliance will remain indispensable for Australian national security even while Australia looks to build a more self-reliant defence force.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks at the National Press Club.

items.title: How to tell if your dog is in pain and what to do if they are

children.content: New research suggests that many dog owners are not skilled in recognising pain in their pets.

gallery.altText: A brown dog with floppy ears.

thumbnail.altText: A brown dog with floppy ears.

items.title: French president calls for ban on social media telling kids to read

children.content: Emmanuel Macron calls for a social media ban for children under 15, a monthly screen-free day for all, and urges high school students to switch off their phones.

image.altText: The french president in a grey suit with the french flag behind him

items.title: Bombs, rain and destruction: Life in Gaza six months into a ceasefire

children.content: Palestinians in Gaza fear they are being forgotten as the Iran war drags on, with Israeli strikes continuing despite a ceasefire and the humanitarian situation still dire.

image.altText: A woman wearing a hijab sits on a chair in front of tents  on dirt.

items.title: End of school holidays sees sunshine in south-east Queensland, rainfall in north

children.content: The south-east corner will see slightly warmer temperatures and sunshine until Sunday, however, localised heavy falls are on the cards for parts of the central interior and north-west.

image.altText: Drone shot of houses spread along a hill before an oceanfront with a mountain the background

items.title: Report finds Darwin Harbour biodiversity rivals that of Ningaloo Reef

children.content: New research has revealed Darwin Harbour's biodiversity, including its wide range of rare species, rivals that of the World Heritage-protected Ningaloo Reef.

image.altText: A drone shot over the harbour, with a manta ray swimming through the water below.

gallery.altText: A drone shot over the harbour, with a manta ray swimming through the water below.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot over the harbour, with a manta ray swimming through the water below.

items.title: Greyhounds ban a sticking point for Liberals and a sore point for Labor

children.content: The ban on greyhound racing in Tasmania was part of a deal made to keep the Rockliff Liberals in power. But getting that ban past parliament is proving to be a mongrel of a job. Meanwhile, Labor seems in denial over the issue.

image.altText: A close up of a greyhound with a hand stroking under its chin.

items.title: Russia launches deadliest attack on Ukraine this year, killing 17

children.content: Russian forces have attacked the Ukrainian capital Kyiv and other cities, killing
people, as the defence leaders of more than 50 countries say they are aiming to send more aid to Ukraine.

image.altText: Two firefighters work as huge orange flames and black smoke fill the night sky, at a recycling centre.
